rationale: The unknown is the standard two-forall trigger gap on `gap`: run-1's ensures triggers on `post1_self_.gap(lo, hi)` and run-2's on `post2_self_.gap(lo, hi)`, so z3 never lines them up. An `assert forall|lo, hi| post1_self_.gap(lo, hi) == post2_self_.gap(lo, hi) by { }` forces both instantiations at matching `(lo, hi)`, after which the view equality `post1_self_@ == post2_self_@` falls out by transitivity through `pre_self_@.insert(k, v)`.
